### Idea 1
- **ID:** I296
- **Title:** Why Your Ears Feel Full After 60 — The Blood Vessel in Your Head (60+)
- **Title (short):** Ears Feel Full After 60? The Blood Vessel in Your Head
- **Pillar:** Vascular / ENT / Body Signals
- **Hook Type:** Misdirection + hidden vascular mechanism (not wax, not infection — blood flow)

- **Original Insight:** "The Internal Carotid Siphon Compression" framework — age-related vascular tortuosity causes the carotid artery to compress the Eustachian tube's blood supply, creating a sensation of fullness that ENT exams miss because they look at the eardrum, not the vessel feeding it.

- **Original Insight:** "Nocturnal Femoral Perfusion Drop" framework — the femoral artery's branch to the hip joint (medial circumflex) experiences reduced flow during sleep due to positional compression + nocturnal BP drop. ### Idea 7
- **ID:** I302
- **Title:** Why Your Fingers Stiffen After 60 — The Joint Lubricant That Disappears (60+)
- **Title (short):** Fingers Stiffen After 60? The Joint Lubricant Disappears
- **Pillar:** Vascular / Joint Health / Mechanism
- **Hook Type:** Common symptom + hidden mechanism reveal + misdirection (not arthritis)
- **Target Emotion:** Frustration recognition → curiosity → empowerment
- **Competitor Gap:** I038/I048 cover cartilage + K2. I060 covers knee cracking. NO ONE covers finger joint stiffness as synovial fluid vascular supply issue. Completely open lane. Universal experience for 60+.
- **Original Insight:** "Synovial Perfusion Pump" framework — synovial fluid is produced by the synovial membrane, which is avascular and relies entirely on diffusion from surrounding capillaries. When capillary density drops after 60, synovial fluid production falls → joints stiffen. Morning stiffness isn't inflammation — it's overnight fluid production deficit. 3-minute hand warming protocol restores production.
- **Score:** 18
- **Status:** PENDING_SCRIPT

- **Original Insight:** "Morning Surge Phenotype" framework — a subset of seniors experience a pathological BP spike at 4 AM (distinct from the physiological dawn phenomenon). This surge is driven by sympathetic nervous system overactivation and is the #1 predictor of morning strokes. 24-hour ABPM reveals it. Dr. Kane's vascular medicine framing makes this actionable.

### Idea 9
- **ID:** I304
- **Title:** Why Your Eyelids Droop After 60 — The Nerve Blood Supply Nobody Checks (60+)
- **Title (short):** Eyelids Droop After 60? The Nerve Blood Supply
- **Pillar:** Vascular / Neurological / Body Signals
- **Hook Type:** Visible body change + hidden vascular mechanism + misdirection (not "just aging")
- **Target Emotion:** Vanity concern + curiosity → understanding → actionable hope
- **Competitor Gap:** I083 covers eyelid twitching (magnesium/nerve). I304 covers eyelid DROOPING (ptosis) as oculomotor nerve microvascular ischemia — completely different mechanism. ZERO competition on ptosis as vascular signal.
- **Original Insight:** "Oculomotor Microvascular Ischemia" framework — the oculomotor nerve's superior branch (levator palpebrae) is supplied by end-arteries with no collateral circulation. When microvascular perfusion drops, this nerve branch is the first to fail → eyelid drooping. It's a canary-in-the-coal-mine for cerebral microvascular health. 3 specific blood tests to request.
- **Score:** 17
- **Status:** PENDING_SCRIPT

### Idea 10
- **ID:** I305
- **Title:** Your Ankles Darken After 60 — The Vein Truth Your Doctor Misses (60+)
- **Title (short):** Ankles Darken After 60? The Vein Truth Doctors Miss
- **Pillar:** Vascular / Dermatological / Diagnostic Gap
- **Hook Type:** Visible body change + hidden vascular mechanism + medical system gap
- **Target Emotion:** Concern + distrust of dismissed symptoms → empowerment through understanding
- **Competitor Gap:** I007/I041 cover swollen ankles (liver/heart). I068 covers bruising (collagen). NO ONE covers ankle hyperpigmentation (hemosiderin staining) as venous insufficiency marker. Completely open lane. Venous stasis dermatitis affects 20%+ of seniors.
- **Original Insight:** "Hemosiderin Sentinel" framework — ankle darkening isn't cosmetic. It's hemosiderin deposition from red blood cells leaking through incompetent venous valves. The medial ankle is the first location because it's where the highest venous pressure meets the thinnest skin. This is visible evidence of chronic venous insufficiency — and it appears YEARS before swelling or ulceration. Compression protocol + when to see vascular specialist.
- **Score:** 19
- **Status:** PENDING_SCRIPT
